# Implementation Plan: AI Tools Support

**Branch**: `011-ai-tools-support` | **Date**: 2026-05-19 | **Spec**: [.specify/specs/011-ai-tools-support/requirements.md](.specify/specs/011-ai-tools-support/requirements.md)  
**Input**: Specification from `.specify/specs/011-ai-tools-support/requirements.md`

**Note**: This plan is produced by `/speckit.plan` for Feature 022 (`AI Tools Support`). It plans the cross-assistant initialization and coexistence model; implementation tasks will be generated by `/speckit.tasks`.

## Summary

Make Spec Kit initialization support every officially supported AI tool with equivalent core workflow coverage, not just GitHub Copilot. The implementation will centralize assistant support metadata, define reusable assistant asset profiles, update `specify init` and refresh behavior so existing `.specify` core files are preserved, generate missing tool-specific assets without overwriting user-maintained content, and add audits/tests that verify multi-tool coexistence and support-surface consistency across CLI help, templates, docs, package resources, and generated workspaces.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Python >=3.8 for CLI package code; Bash for project workflow scripts; Markdown/YAML for templates, commands, instructions, and contracts.  
**Primary Dependencies**: Typer, Rich, httpx[socks], platformdirs, readchar, bundled `templates/`, `.specify/scripts/bash/`, `scripts/bash/`, and file-system utilities from the Python standard library.  
**Storage**: File-based project assets under `.specify/`, `.github/`, `.claude/`, `.qwen/`, `.opencode/`, `.qoder/`, root compatibility instruction files, and generated documentation; no database storage.  
**Testing**: pytest with existing `tests/contract`, `tests/integration`, and `tests/unit`; shell behavior validated via repository scripts and fixture workspaces.  
**Target Platform**: Cross-platform CLI distribution with primary validation on Linux/macOS and compatible behavior for Windows where supported by the package; generated Bash workflow assets remain the supported script variant.  
**Project Type**: Single Python CLI package with bundled templates, scripts, docs, memory, skills, and SDD artifacts.  
**Performance Goals**: New-project or existing-workspace assistant initialization completes within normal CLI initialization time; support summary can be read in under 1 minute; release audit covers 100% of official assistants and canonical command templates.  
**Constraints**: Existing initialized `.specify` core files must not be overwritten by template defaults; tool-specific assets must not become independent workflow sources of truth; existing assistant integrations must remain intact when another tool is added; compatibility symlinks must continue pointing to canonical `.specify` assets where applicable.  
**Scale/Scope**: Official assistants listed by the project today: GitHub Copilot, Claude Code, Qwen Code, opencode, and Qoder. Scope covers CLI selection, asset generation, existing-project refresh, preserve/skip/summary behavior, docs, templates, tests, packaging, and Feature 022 tracking.

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

The repository constitution is active and authoritative. This plan evaluates gates directly against the Spec Kit Constitution, including specification traceability, Feature 022 linkage and governance, intent clarity, test-first implementation, official AI agent constraints, continuous quality, and the specification-plan-task-implementation workflow.

**Core Principles Compliance**:

- **Specification-Driven Development**: Pass. The plan traces directly to FR-001 through FR-012, user stories, edge cases, and success criteria.
- **Feature-Centric Development**: Pass. Requirements are bound to Feature 022, and related assistant-specific features remain separate dependencies rather than being merged.
- **Intent-Driven Development**: Pass. The plan preserves the user intent to support all official AI tools, support coexistence, and reuse initialized `.specify` core files.
- **Test-First & Contract-Driven**: Pass. Contract, integration, and unit test targets are defined before implementation tasks.
- **AI Agent Integration**: Pass. Scope is limited to officially approved AI agents: Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Qwen Code, opencode, and Qoder; the design keeps `.specify` as the canonical workflow source and generates assistant compatibility surfaces from shared templates/instructions.
- **Continuous Quality & Observability**: Pass. Initialization summaries and release audits are first-class outputs.
- **SDD Workflow Compliance**: Pass. Requirements and clarification are complete; this plan generates Phase 0/1 artifacts and hands off to `/speckit.tasks`.

**Gates Status**: ✅ All gates pass for planning against the restored Spec Kit Constitution. No unresolved clarifications or unjustified constitutional violations remain.

```text

├── scripts/

├── templates/

templates/

│   └── *.md

├── usage.md

tests/

├── integration/
```

**Structure Decision**: Use the existing single Python CLI structure. Cross-assistant behavior belongs primarily in `src/specify_cli/__init__.py`; script/runtime path fixes and instruction refresh belong in `scripts/bash/` and `.specify/scripts/bash/`; canonical command content remains under `templates/commands/` and `.specify/templates/commands/`; validation belongs in `tests/contract`, `tests/integration`, and `tests/unit`.

## Complexity Tracking

N/A. The selected design extends existing assistant-support patterns rather than adding new application services or storage layers.

## Phase 0: Research Review & Context

- Reviewed `README.md`, `docs/installation.md`, `docs/usage.md`, `docs/quickstart.md`, `docs/spec-driven.md`, `docs/vibe-coding.md`, `docs/security.md`, `docs/upstream.md`, and `docs/skills/*` for initialization, supported-assistant claims, skills compatibility, SDD workflow expectations, and safety guidance.
- Reviewed `.specify/memory/features.md` and `.specify/memory/features/*.md` summaries. Feature 020 (Qoder Support) and Feature 021 (Claude Code Support) are closest implementation patterns; Feature 008 (Instructions Command), Feature 015 (CLI Interface), Feature 017 (Template Engine), Feature 019 (Agents Command), and Feature 013 (Skills Command) provide integration boundaries.
- Inspected `pyproject.toml`, `src/specify_cli/__init__.py`, `.specify/scripts/bash/common.sh`, `.specify/scripts/bash/create-new-plan.sh`, tests for Claude/Qoder support, and existing contracts to identify reusable assistant metadata, command generation, skill symlink, refresh, and support-surface audit patterns.
- No prior `research.md` existed for this spec. Decisions and rationale are captured in the generated `research.md` artifact.

**Phase 0 Output**: `research.md`

## Phase 1: Design & Contracts

### Design Decisions

1. **Central assistant support profile**
   - Use the existing assistant matrix as the canonical machine-readable list of official assistant keys, display names, folders, install guidance, and CLI validation requirements.
   - Extend generated summaries and audits from that matrix instead of maintaining parallel support lists in individual tool paths.

2. **Shared core preservation policy**
   - Treat `.specify/memory`, `.specify/templates`, `.specify/scripts`, `.specify/skills`, and `.specify/instructions.md` as canonical core workspace assets.
   - During existing-project initialization or refresh, copy only missing core assets by default; skip initialized core files and report them as reused/preserved.
   - Existing core files with user changes are never overwritten unless a future explicit force/merge path is chosen by the user.

3. **Tool-specific asset isolation**
   - Generate assistant-specific commands, instruction compatibility files, settings, ignore files, and skills links under each assistant’s own root.
   - Assistant-specific assets may reference canonical `.specify` files but must not redefine the workflow independently.
   - Adding one assistant must not delete, rewrite, or downgrade another assistant’s root.

4. **Repeatable result summary and audits**
   - Initialization and refresh should return a structured user-visible summary covering created, reused, skipped, preserved, conflict, and attention-required items.
   - Release audits compare official assistants with CLI help, README/docs, templates, generated command coverage, instruction links, packaged resources, and tests.

5. **Script path consistency**
   - Workflow scripts must resolve current specs under `.specify/specs/<requirements-key>/` and refer to `requirements.md` consistently.
   - The plan run exposed a local script inconsistency in `.specify/scripts/bash/common.sh`; this is treated as a supporting fix needed for reliable SDD commands and should be covered by regression tests.

### Planned Design Artifacts

- `research.md`: Planning decisions, rejected alternatives, and integration rationale.
- `data-model.md`: Defines `AssistantSupportProfile`, `CoreWorkspaceAsset`, `ToolSpecificAssetSet`, `InitializationOperation`, `InitializationResultSummary`, and `SupportSurfaceAudit`.
- `contracts/ai-tools-support.openapi.yaml`: Contract for assistant listing, project initialization, existing-project assistant refresh, core asset preservation, command coverage, and support audits.
- `quickstart.md`: Validation scenarios for new-project setup, existing-project tool addition, preservation, coexistence, repeat runs, and audit coverage.
- `feature-ref.md`: Feature relationship review for `/speckit.tasks`.

## Constitution Check (Post-Design Re-check)

- **Specification-Driven Development**: Pass. Design artifacts trace to all user stories, FR-001 through FR-012, and SC-001 through SC-006.
- **Feature-Centric Development**: Pass. Feature 022 is the primary feature; Features 020 and 021 are implementation references, not replacements, and Feature 022 remains Planned until implementation tasks are executed and validated.
- **Intent-Driven Development**: Pass. The plan focuses on user-visible initialization parity and preservation rather than premature internal rewrites.
- **Test-First & Contract-Driven**: Pass. The contract and quickstart define acceptance before task generation.
- **AI Agent Integration**: Pass. Multi-agent compatibility remains derived from canonical Spec Kit assets and is limited to the constitution-approved assistant set.
- **Continuous Quality & Observability**: Pass. Result summaries and support audits make behavior inspectable.
- **SDD Workflow Compliance**: Pass. Requirements, clarifications, plan, research, data model, contract, quickstart, and feature reference are present for task generation.

**Post-Design Gates Status**: ✅ All gates pass against the restored Spec Kit Constitution. No unresolved technical context remains.

## Phase 2: Implementation Planning

1. Refactor assistant support metadata so every official assistant can be enumerated, validated, initialized, refreshed, and audited from one source of truth.
2. Extend `copy_local_templates()` and related init paths to distinguish canonical `.specify` core assets from tool-specific assets, preserving existing initialized core files by default.
3. Add per-assistant asset generation strategies for Copilot, Claude Code, Qwen Code, opencode, and Qoder so each receives full command and instruction coverage.
4. Add result-summary collection for initialization and refresh with created/reused/skipped/preserved/conflict/attention-required categories.
5. Add coexistence protections so refreshing one assistant cannot remove or rewrite other assistant roots or user-maintained tool-specific files.
6. Align `.specify/scripts/bash/` path helpers with the `.specify/specs/<requirements-key>/requirements.md` layout and cover it with regression tests.
7. Update README, docs, templates, CLI help, and distribution packaging audits so support claims match the assistant matrix.
8. Add contract, integration, and unit tests for new-project initialization, existing-project addition, core preservation, tool-specific isolation, repeat-run idempotence, and support-surface coverage.
9. Update Feature 022 memory and index during implementation/review as artifacts mature.
